description: This sparkling wine starts with a toasty but soft nose, leading into clean but fruity flavors and a refreshing finish. Though an earlier release might have allowed more primary fruit to show through, the wine offers easygoing but elegant enjoyment.
title: Hermann J. Wiemer 2002 Blanc de Blanc Chardonnay (Finger Lakes)

description: Strongly oaked, but fresh, young and healthy in the nose. The smell of coconut and lemon curd is friendly and soft, while the palate is loaded with more lemon, coconut, pear and nutmeg. The texture is rich, and, for now, the acidity is bright. A solid Chardonnay for current drinking.
title: San Pedro 2002 Castillo de Molina Reserva Chardonnay (Lontué Valley)

description: Bright, fresh, fruity and light bodied, with pretty peach, apple and spice flavors that leave a crisp, clean and pleasant finish. Fairly sweet, but refreshing.
title: J. Lohr 2002 Bay Mist Riesling (Monterey)

description: In acknowledgement of the superior quality of 2002, Moët has released this wine well after the 2003. The decision is fully justified, for this is still young. It is impressive, intensely concentrated, mineral, showing tight grapefruit and apple flavors. Excellent aging potential.
title: Moët & Chandon 2002 Grand Vintage Brut  (Champagne)

description: This Cabernet Sauvignon-Merlot blend almost has enough of the right stuff to rate higher, but in the end the wine's tart cherry fruit and sharp acidity is just too unfriendly. Like so many Brazilian wines, it's trying to be more than the country's terroir can give. So the end result is mediocre.
title: Château Lacave 2002 Reserva Cabernet Sauvignon-Merlot (Serra Gaúcha)

description: This powerful expression of Riesling blends floral aromas with scents of lime, pineapple and crushed stone, then finishes with great length and intensity. The strident citrus and mineral notes seem to echo on the palate, filling the mouth with their characterful flavors.
title: Felton Road 2002 Dry Riesling (Central Otago)

description: Smells considerably riper than most Otago Rieslings, with nectarine and apricot aromas giving way to a thickly-textured wine with bold stone-fruit flavors and just enough citrusy acidity on the finish to keep the wine balanced. Most comparable in style to off-dry Alsatian Rieslings, possibly even vendanges tardives wines.
title: Pegasus Bay 2002 Riesling (Waipara)

description: Tahbilk, one of Australia's historic wineries, has turned out a velvety-textured Shiraz named for one of the company's long-time winemakers. This is a medium-bodied, yet velvety-soft Shiraz that boasts plenty of intensity. If its blackberry, licorice and vanilla flavors develop more complexity over the next few years, the 89 rating may seem stingy.
title: Tahbilk 2002 Eric Stevens Purbrick Shiraz (Nagambie Lakes)

description: Offers cracker and wheat aromas, and light white stone-fruit flavors that seem coated in confectioner's sugar, or talc. Perhaps it's the “unwooded” designation talking, but this wine seems a little light, and not much like Chardonnay.
title: Wakefield 2002 Promised Land - Unwooded Chardonnay (South Australia)

description: This older vintage of a hot-climate Merlot is almost past its prime. The wine appears old and delivers aged notes of resin, tar and asphalt with little fruit. It has a brownish hue and tastes spicy and gritty.
title: Feudo Principi di Butera 2002 Calat Merlot (Sicilia)

description: Lean and racy, with limes and tart green apples playing major roles. If not for a dollop of sugar, it might seem a bit sour, but as it is it comes across as pleasantly tart and properly acidic.
title: Shelton Vineyards 2002 Riesling (North Carolina)

description: The top range of wines from this highly rated cooperative is called Sanet Valentin, after the patron saint of lovers. This Sauvignon is crisp, herbaceous and very fresh, with light layers of mouth-watering acidity.
title: San Michele Eppan 2002 Sanct Valentin Sauvignon Blanc (Alto Adige)

description: An austere blend of Cab Sauvignon, Syrah and Petit Verdot. There's color and power galore, but also a choppy, firm mouthfeel that guards intense cherry flavors and smoldering oak. An aggressive wine that doesn't back down from its essence. Will do much better with food than by itself.
title: Marques de Griñon 2002 Estate Bottled Summa Varietalis Red (Dominio de Valdepusa)

description: A full-bodied, rounded wine with some good crisp acidity to balance. It has flavors of pears and some spice, leaving an attractive fresh feel in the mouth.
title: Cantina Tramin 2002 Pinot Grigio (Alto Adige)

description: A fun, youthful wine. Juicy, chewy fruit on the palate isn't overpowered by lots of oak. Ripe plum and blackberry on the nose; finishes with less verve than the palate has.
title: D'Arenberg 2002 d'Arry's Original Shiraz-Grenache (McLaren Vale)

description: A spicy, creamy wine that manages to give freshness and intense varietal character at the same time. The overall effect is of lightness and elegance as well as spiciness and richness.
title: J. Hofstatter 2002 Kolbenhof Gewürztraminer (Alto Adige)

description: This could very well be Chile's most expressive, interesting white Sauvignon. It's got all those prickly pineapple, passion fruit, scallion and pepper notes that are typical, and also a lot of pure citrus flavors backing it all up. The mouthfeel is zesty and spirited, but there's natural roundness, too. Some licorice adds complexity to the finish. Best Buy.
title: Montes 2002 Reserve Sauvignon Blanc (Casablanca Valley)

description: Smoky and roasted on the nose, it also has standard red-berry aromas lurking in the background. This feels fairly full and healthy, with sweet-oak flavors accenting lightly-pickled-tasting red berry. It finishes a bit herbal, with lasting minty, oaky flavors.
title: Bodegas Virgen del Águila 2002 Señorío del Águila Reserva Red (Cariñena)

description: Understated stone fruit and mineral flavors dominate the palate, with peach-pit and citrus notes on the finish. Round and resinous in the mouth; nose offers a white peppery, plasticy bite.
title: Penfolds 2002 Reserve Bin Riesling (Eden Valley)

description: Made entirely with Nosiola, this opens with aromas of dried apricot, crème caramel and a whiff of orange jam. The rounded palate delivers orange blossom honey, fig and a hint of toasted almond.
title: Pisoni 2002 Vino Santo Nosiola (Trentino)

description: An interesting wine, at a good price: Has pastry dough, lime and yellow fruit aromas, and a slightly viscous texture. In the mouth, stone fruit wears a minerally cloak. White pepper and herb flavors add zip to the finish.
title: Redbank 2002 The Long Paddock Chardonnay (Victoria)

description: Weinert has always pushed the more traditional, less extracted style. And this Bordeaux blend shows a nose of light spice, cinnamon, wet clay and subdued berry fruit. The palate is earthy, with hints of tomato, herbs, raspberry and cocoa. It's a drying wine, with grabby tannins, but it should age well for another few years. Drink now or hold through 2011.
title: Weinert 2002 Gran Vino Red (Mendoza)

description: A good choice for fans of older wines, the Raphael 2002 Merlot shows mature forest floor and earth scents but is still hanging on to some raspberry and blackberry fruit. The crisp palate has a mouthwatering character and a touch of singed plum flavor. Woody tannins chime in on the medium finish. Drink before 2011.
title: Raphael 2002 Estate Bottled Merlot (North Fork of Long Island)

description: A soft, almond-flavored wine that has fresh, green and ripe fruit. The blend, with small amounts of Chardonnay and Trebbiano, gives a feeling of fullness and roundness and is already ready for drinking.
title: Bertani 2002  Soave Classico Superiore

description: An easy, full-flavored wine, with crisp, clean fruit, flavors of almonds and a touch of soft,ripeness.
title: Masi 2002 Classico  (Soave)

description: This wine from the Tiefenbrunner estate at Castel Turmhof is a lively, crisp, fresh style of wine, with flavors of green peppers and mint, and good acidity.
title: Tiefenbrunner 2002 Kirchleiten Sauvignon Blanc (Alto Adige)

description: Rustic, smoky and deep on the nose, with a creamy body holding up commendable raspberry and plum flavors. Valenciso deals medium-level acids and tannins, with oak-based mocha and chocolate carrying the finish. This wine is so close to reaching the next level, but it doesn't quite get there.
title: Valenciso 2002 Reserva  (Rioja)

description: Earth, leather and tomato are accents to the dry, lean bouquet. The wine is expectedly juicy and tight, with snappy raspberry and cherry flavors. It shows clean lines and an unbreakable personality; elegant, juicy and firm. Drink now–2009.
title: Bodegas Faustino 2002 Faustino V Reserva  (Rioja)

description: An elegant, charming wine that offers structure as well as finesse. The fruits are delicious, perfumed, just maturing into smoky bacon flavors. Dry tannins and red fruits are balanced by fresh acidity.
title: Domaine des Lambrays 2002  Clos de Lambrays

description: Here's a wine that is meandering towards maturity, its green herbal character leaning towards some steeliness, minerality and toast. It has richness, still possesses freshness, but also considerable delicacy. Screwcap.
title: Hirsch 2002 Zöbinger Heiligenstein Riesling (Kamptal)

description: Pear and apple aromas receive a kick from some creamy, vanilla-tinged oak. The palate is mostly tangerine and lime, with some rich baked-apple undertones. The lengthy finish is solid, as is the mouthfeel. Best Buy.
title: Carmen 2002 Classic Chardonnay (Central Valley)

description: Fruit's a bit thin, tending to tart green apples, grapefruit and lime, and oak plays a leading role, lending notes of smoke and vanilla and a creamy texture. Very well structured. You find yourself wishing there was more depth of flavor.
title: Ferrari-Carano 2002 Fumé Blanc (Sonoma County)
